steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content:
A radius to a tangent point is perpendicular to the tangent. Since the tangent slope is 
$$
\frac{4}{7}
$$, the radius slope is
$$-\frac{7}{4}$$

steps.title: Write the radius slope
steps.content:
Using the center $(3,8)$ and tangent point $(a,-5)$, the radius slope is
$$\frac{-5-8}{a-3}=\frac{-13}{a-3}$$

steps.title: Solve for the coordinate
steps.content: Set the two slope expressions equal and solve. $$\frac{-13}{a-3}=-\frac{7}{4}$$ $$-52=-7(a-3)$$ $$a=\frac{73}{7}$$ The answer is D.
